threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’re still tossing out 'brekkie' instead of just saying breakfast and eyeing a proper flat white like it’s a foreign artifact.
Last night on the 196, saw a guy in a sequined jacket doing a one-man show. Unbothered, the rest of us just sipped our 2-for-1 cans. Classic Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw the elderly couple setting up a small table outside their building every Sunday to sell homemade jams and pickles. Just so Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a Turkish market selling fresh simit and a bar with homemade Kölsch right across the street. Totally unique vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they groan about the new 'artisanal' burrito place taking over the old taqueria. We know what’s real.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus yesterday with a full mariachi band on board. Everyone just smiled and clapped along. Only in the Mission, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and hip bars, but the real charm is in the quiet plazas where locals gather for 2pm coffee.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them at the Mercat de Sant Josep, staring wide-eyed at the stalls, fumbling with their euros like it’s a foreign currency.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the De Beauvoir block is trendy and not just overpriced. The locals can spot a newbie by where they choose to brunch.
Caught a glimpse of a guy serenading a traffic cone on the 35 last night. The cone didn’t sing back, but it did get a round of applause from the drunks.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I stumbled upon a tiny café with an old Turkish guy playing backgammon with locals. The smell of fresh baklava wafting out hit me—this is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop where the owner insists on playing classical music while he slices the döner. Adds a touch of elegance to late-night hunger.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the 'new' taqueria on Valencia is actually the same old spot with a fresh coat of paint. Tourists go wild for it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 14 bus passengers with a ukulele last week. Perfectly in tune with the chaos of the traffic jam. Only in the Mission.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all of Gràcia is just bohemian cafés, but they miss the real gems—like the old bocadillo bars hiding in plain sight.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them in Gràcia, holding a map upside down while trying to find a tapas bar. The locals just stroll by with their sangria.
